fallback: No local cangjie skill was available in this runtime. Used book2skill and skill-creator-style distillation, with the fallback recorded here as requested.
metadataNotes: The local metadata identifies the representative work as Leonard Kleinrock, Queueing Systems, Volume I: Theory, but the actual assigned source available for distillation is a MIT web supplement section rather than the book text. Supplemented the skill with standard queueing concepts needed for executable orchestrator design and also present in local pack notes: lambda arrival rate, mu service rate, c worker count, rho utilization, Little's Law, queue discipline, priority separation, backpressure, retry budgets, and observability metrics.
supplementedFrom: General stable queueing-theory concepts used to make the skill operational for auto-orchestrator design

closureReview.scope: Closed-loop review of SKILL.md, test-prompts.json, and audit.json.

qualityChecks.standaloneKnowledge: SKILL.md defines queue boundary, arrival/service measurement, model fidelity, operating region, policy, instrumentation, recovery, and boundaries without requiring source files.
qualityChecks.runtimeDependencyPolicy: External files, webpages, original books, source reports, external source folder, distilled source material, and out-of-directory material are provenance only and not runtime dependencies.
qualityChecks.triggerContract: Trigger, non-trigger, activation gate, and execution gate are explicit.
qualityChecks.workflowAndDeliverables: Workflow, queueing analysis deliverables, failure/recovery/idempotency, and hard rules are present.
qualityChecks.testPromptCoverage: test-prompts.json includes should_trigger, should_not_trigger, edge_cases, and compliance_expectations.

remainingRisks: Representative book text was not directly present, but all runtime queueing-theory concepts needed by the skill are compressed into SKILL.md.

runtimeDependencyPolicy: Closed skill: execute from SKILL.md; use references/source-notes.md only as local optional provenance; do not require external source folders, webpages, original books, source reports, machine-local paths, or cross-skill files.
